data.title: DIIs net buy shares worth Rs 3,620 crore, FIIs net sell Rs 5,062-crore shares
data.description:
In the year so far, FIIs have net sold shares worth Rs 2.36 lakh crore, while DIIs have bought Rs 5.11 lakh crore shares.

On October 24, Domestic Institutional Investors (DII) net bought shares worth Rs 3,620 crore. On the other hand, Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) net sold shares worth Rs 5,062 crore, provisional data from NSE showed.

DIIs bought Rs 13,828 crore worth of shares and sold shares worth Rs 10,207 crore. Meanwhile, FIIs purchased Rs 12,537-crore shares and offloaded equities worth Rs 17,600 crore during the trading session.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In the year so far, FIIs have net sold shares worth Rs 2.36 lakh crore, while DIIs have bought Rs 5.11 lakh crore shares.

Market view

At close, the Sensex was down 138.74 points or 0.17 percent at 80,081.98, and the Nifty was down 36.60 points or 0.15 percent at 24,435.50.

Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Auto, Tech Mahindra, HDFC Bank, Tata Consumer were among the top gainers on the Nifty, while losers included M&M, Sun Pharma, Eicher Motors, Power Grid Corp and L&T.

Among sectors, IT index up more than 2 percent, while capital goods, power, pharma down 1 percent each.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to  Ajit Mishra – SVP, Research, Religare Broking Ltd, the past two days of stagnation in the Nifty index reflect indecision following a recent downturn, though the overall sentiment remains negative. "Some oversold heavyweight stocks may prompt a short-term bounce, but the upside appears limited. Investors are advised to maintain a “sell on rise” strategy for the index while being selective with individual stocks," Mishra said,

data.title: SME Update: Danish Power, United Heat Transfer IPOs see robust subscription; OBSC Perfection issue gets 15.5x bids
data.description:
IPO

In the SME segment, three public issues, namely Danish Power, United Heat Transfer, and OBSC Perfection, closed for subscription on October 24, while the Usha Financial Services IPO was fully subscribed on its first day of bidding. All of them are from the NSE Emerge.

Amongst them, Danish Power, the biggest-ever SME IPO, recorded 117.9 times subscription with investors buying 44.14 crore equity shares as against the offer size of 37.44 lakh shares, the subscription data on the exchanges showed.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Jaipur-based transformers maker opened its 52.08 lakh shares initial share sale on October 22 to raise Rs 197.9 crore at the upper end of price band of Rs 360-380 per share.

Danish Power already raised Rs 55.63 crore from 19 anchor investors on October 21, a day before the IPO opening. Bharat Venture Opportunities Fund, Carnelian Structural Shift Fund, Finavenue Capital Trust, JM Financial Products, Niveshaay Hedgehogs Fund, Tattvam AIF Trust, Valuequest India, and Visionary Value Fund were some of the anchor investors.

With two manufacturing plants in Jaipur, the company supplies transformers to several power and power related companies including Tata Power Solar System, Waaree Renewable Technologies, Jakson Green, ABB India, and Torrent Power.

The initial public offering of United Heat Transfer was subscribed 77.9 times on Thursday, with investors bidding for 28.43 crore shares as against the issue size of 36.5 lakh shares.

The heat exchangers, pressure vessels, and process flow skids maker targets to mobilise Rs 30 crore through its IPO, which comprises solely fresh issue. The price band for the offer was Rs 56-59 per share.

The Nashik-based company's products find applications in petrol and diesel engines, maritime vessels, mining trucks, and heavy machinery segments.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Precision metal components manufacturer OBSC Perfection recorded 15.46 times subscription as investors bought 7.3 crore equity shares against the IPO size of 47.23 lakh shares. The price band for the issue was 95-100 per share.

The company, which primarily caters to original equipment manufacturers that ultimately supply various components and parts to top automotive manufacturing companies of India, opened its public issue on October 22 to raise Rs 66.02 crore which will be mainly used for purchase of machinery for the existing manufacturing facilities, and working capital requirements.

Non-banking finance company Usha Financial Services opened its Rs 98.5-crore maiden public issue, which closed its first day of bidding with 1.24 times subscription. Investors pumped in money for 52.14 lakh shares as against the offer size of 41.99 lakh shares. The issue, which has a price band of Rs 160-168 per share, will close next week on October 28.

Grey Market Premium

Danish Power, United Heat Transfer, and OBSC Perfection will finalise the basis of allotment of their IPO shares by October 25. They will credit IPO shares to the demat accounts of successful investors by October 28.

Investors can start trading in equity shares of these companies on the NSE Emerge, effective October 29.

Amongst these four, Danish Power IPO shares have been attracting strong interest in the grey market, trading at around 70-75 percent premium over the upper price band, while United Heat Transfer, and OBSC Perfection saw 30-40 percent and 15-20 percent premium, the market observers said.

data.title: Axis Bank re-appoints Amitabh Chaudhry as MD and CEO for three years
data.description:
Axis Bank has re-appointed Amitabh Chaudhry as the MD and CEO of the bank for another three years with effect from January 2025.

Axis Bank has re-appointed Amitabh Chaudhry as the MD and CEO of the bank for another three years with effect from January 2025, up to December 31, 2027.

The re-appointment was subject to the approval of the Reserve Bank of India. Shareholders of Axis Bank had approved the decision at the 30th Annual General Meeting on July 26 this year.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"We now wish to inform you that the Reserve Bank of India has approved the re-appointment of Amitabh Chaudhry as the Managing Director & CEO of the Bank, for a period of three years w.e.f. January 1, 2025 till December 31, 2027 (both days inclusive)," Axis Bank said in an exchange filing on October 24.

This is being updated
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Research reports of RAs will not be considered an advertisement, Sebi clarifies
data.description:
The report will only be considered an ad only if it promotes products or services by the RA, said the Sebi circular.

The market regulator has clarified that research reports and research recommendations released by a research analyst (RA) will not be considered an advertisement.

The report will only be considered an ad only if it promotes products or services by the RA, said a circular issued by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) on Thursday, October 24.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

According to a circular, there is some confusion around the applicability of the advertising code on research reports. Therefore, the regulator issued a clarification.

The circular said, "SEBI is in receipt of certain queries with respect to applicability of provisions of advertisement code on a Research Report issued by an RA. In this regard, it is clarified that Research Report and research recommendations of an RA are not considered advertisement unless anything contained in the research report is in the nature of promotion of products or services offered by an RA."

Also read: Explained: Why a merchant banker's 'novel' way of raising its networth didn't fly with SEBI

"Accordingly, the paragraph 8.1 a. ii. of the Master Circular shall read as under:
“The forms of communications, to which the advertisement code shall be applicable, shall include pamphlets, circulars, brochures, notices or any other literature, document, information or material published, or designed for use in any publication or displays (such as newspaper, magazine, sign boards/hoardings at any location), in any electronic, wired or wireless communication (such as electronic mail, text messaging, messaging platforms, social media platforms, radio, telephone, or in any other form over the internet) or over any other audio-visual form of communication (such as television, tape recording, video tape recordings, motion pictures) or in any other manner whatsoever.

It stated, "Further, a research report, irrespective of the mode of its dissemination to any investor or prospective investor, shall be construed as an advertisement if anything contained in the said research report is either expressly or impliedly in the nature of promotion of products or services offered by an RA.”
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Technical View: Bulls may return and drive Nifty towards 24,500-24,600, but negative trend raises doubts over sustainability
data.description:
Nifty Rangebound

The Nifty 50 closed the rangebound session moderately lower, extending its downward journey for the fourth consecutive day on October 24. The index traded within a 50-60 point range during the day and formed a Doji candlestick pattern (not a classical one), following an Inverted Hammer-like pattern in the previous session. The formation of such patterns after a downtrend generally indicates the possibility of a rebound. If this comes true, the index may face hurdles at the 24,500-24,600 range on the higher side. However, the overall negative trend raises doubts about the sustainability of this bounce, as experts continue to advise a "sell on rally" strategy. On the downside, selling pressure may extend if the index breaks below 24,300.

The Nifty 50 closed at 24,399, down 36 points after hitting an intraday high of 24,481 and a low of 24,341.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Although the short-term trend of the Nifty remains negative, the present pattern formation signals a possibility of an upside bounce from here or from slightly lower levels, according to Nagaraj Shetti, Senior Technical Research Analyst at HDFC Securities. Furthermore, the absence of sharp selling momentum at the lows in the last couple of sessions also indicates chances of an upside bounce, he believes.

According to him, a sustainable move above the 24,600-24,700 levels could confirm the extent of the upside bounce in the market. However, a slide below 24,300 could trigger more weakness in the near term, he added.

The weekly options data indicated that the 24,400 level is likely to be crucial for further market direction; above it, 24,500 may serve as immediate resistance, while below it, 24,300 is the level to watch.

On the Call side, the 24,400 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 24,500 and 25,000 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,400 strike, then the 24,500 and 25,100 strikes. On the Put side, maximum open interest was seen at the 24,400 strike, followed by the 24,000 and 24,300 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,400 strike, then the 24,300 and 23,900 strikes.

Bank Nifty

The Bank Nifty continued to defend the 100-day EMA (Exponential Moving Average - 51,100) for another session and rebounded by 292 points to reach 51,531, snapping a three-day decline. The index formed a bullish candlestick pattern on the daily charts, following a Bullish Inverted Hammer-like pattern in the previous session.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The index faced resistance around the 51,800 – 51,900 levels. The bounce should be used as a selling opportunity. On the downside, we expect the Bank Nifty to drift lower towards 50,500 from a short-term perspective," said Jatin Gedia, Technical Research Analyst at Sharekhan by BNP Paribas.

Volatility dropped after three days of gains, falling slightly below the 14 level. Until it sustains below this mark, the bulls need to be cautious. The India VIX was down by 4.46 percent to 13.97.

data.title: Godavari Biorefineries IPO sees 54% subscription on day 2, retail portion booked 0.96x
data.description:
Godavari Biorefineries IPO

The Rs 555-crore initial share sale of Godavari Biorefineries continued to see muted response from investors even on the second day of bidding, October 24, may be following subdued equity market conditions. The issue has seen 54 percent subscription, with investors buying 60.92 lakh equity shares against the offer size of 1.12 crore shares.

The ethanol-based chemicals maker targets to raise Rs 325 crore via fresh issue component and the remaining funds of Rs 229.75 crore through offer-for-sale (OFS) of 65.26 lakh shares. The price band for the issue is Rs 334-352 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Mandala Capital AG, the only investor in the company, will be exiting the company by selling its entire shareholding of 49.26 lakh shares in the offer-for-sale, while the remaining 16 lakh shares will be offloaded by promoters.

Retail investors bought 0.96 times the allotted quota, while non-institutional investors subscribed for 26 percent shares of the reserved portion set aside for them. Qualified institutional buyers bid for 8,526 equity shares so far, against the reserved portion of 31.45 lakh shares.

Godavari Biorefineries, one of largest producers of ethanol in India, intends to utilise the maximum money of Rs 240 crore out of the fresh issue proceeds for repaying its debt. The remaining funds will be used for general corporate purposes.

The company produces bio-based chemicals, sugar, different grades of ethanol and power. It supplies these products to several industries such as food, beverages, pharmaceuticals, flavours & fragrances, power, fuel, personal care and cosmetics.

It competes with several listed peers like Alkyl Amines Chemicals, Jubilant Ingrevia, Laxmi Organic Industries, EID Parry (India), Triveni Engineering and Industries, Balrampur Chini Mills, Dalmia Bharat Sugar & Industries, Dhampur Sugar Mills, and Dwarikesh Sugar Industries.

According to the market observers, Godavari IPO shares are not trading at any premium in the grey market, which is an unofficial market wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The issue will close for subscription on October 25.

data.title: Nvidia has over 10,000 engineers in India, says CEO Jensen Huang
data.description:
Jensen Huang

Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang disclosed that the company has over 10,000 engineers in India. He said this in a fireside chat with Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani at the Nvidia AI Summit in Mumbai on October 24, discussing AI and India’s role in the nascent technology.

Ambani was speaking on how India can leverage its demographic dividend and hone 2 lakh people in the country to leadership roles. To this, Huang remarked on his company’s India headcount.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Interestingly, a blog post by the company just two days ago read that it has over 4,000 employees in India, across six locations.

In 2023, the world's “hottest chip company” had 3,800 employees in the country across four development centres — Gurugram, Hyderabad, Pune and Bengaluru. And a large chunk of this, over 2,200, were based in India’s Silicon Valley. While speaking to journalists in 2023, Huang had said the company would be looking to double or triple its headcount here but did not offer any timeline.

World over, there is unity in the saying that India has an incredible technology talent pool and it will only further grow in the future. For example, semiconductor company AMD on November 28 unveiled its largest global design centre in Bengaluru, spanning 500,000 square feet and housing around 3,000 engineers.

Meanwhile, Huang’s India visit marks many important developments. Nvidia announced a slew of partnerships just before the CEO was about to deliver a speech. The company announced strategic partnerships with Indian IT firms and other companies at the Summit.

Tata Consultancy Services launched a new Nvidia business unit to speed up the industry-wide adoption of AI by its customers. Huang said service partners from Wipro to Infosys to TCS, are working with Nvidia to take AI models and the AI infrastructure out to the world's enterprises.

Tata Communications also unveiled an AI infrastructure partnership with Nvidia for accelerated computing. Additionally, the company is working with data centre firm Yotta to build a fundamental computing infrastructure in India.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: In charts: Less than 50% of equity diversified funds outperformed their benchmarks in September
data.description:
As per AMFI data for September, open-ended equity mutual fund inflows fell 10 percent (MoM) to Rs 34,419 crore in September. However, inflows into open-ended equity funds stayed in the positive for the 43rd month in a row.

Equity diversified funds seem to be having a tough time amidst the heightened volatility in the markets as the share of schemes that are able to outperform the benchmarks has shrunk in the month of September when compared to the previous month.

According to a report by Prabhudas Lilladher Capital, among the 287 open-ended equity diversified funds, only 48 percent were able to outperform their benchmarks in the month of September -- a significant drop from 67 percent in August.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Over a one-year period, however, 55 percent of the funds outpaced their benchmarks, improving from last year’s 48 percent.

The Nifty 50 TRI index rose 2.28 percent during September, while the Nifty Midcap 150 TRI and Nifty Small Cap 250 TRI posted 1.8 percent and 1.4 percent gains, respectively.

As per AMFI data for September, open-ended equity mutual fund inflows fell 10 percent (MoM) to Rs 34,419 crore in September. However, inflows into open-ended equity funds stayed in the positive for the 43rd month in a row.

Category-wise performance

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In the large-cap category, Motilal Oswal Large Cap Fund led the pack with a 3.85 percent return for the month, followed closely by SBI BlueChip Fund, which delivered returns of 3.18 percent, and Franklin India Bluechip Fund at 3.15 percent. For the full year, the Quant Large Cap Fund topped the list with 45.88 percent returns, followed by Baroda BNP Paribas Large Cap Fund with 45.16 percent.

Among mid-cap funds, Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund registered returns of 5.49 percent in September and around 71.80 percent over the past year. Other top performers include Invesco India Midcap Fund (4.96 percent for the month and 59.93 percent annually) and Aditya Birla SL Midcap Fund (4.91 percent for the month and 47.67 percent for the year).

Among small-cap funds, Axis Small Cap Fund gave returns of 3.84 percent in September, while Bandhan Small Cap Fund saw a 72.13 percent return over the last year. Mahindra Manulife Small Cap Fund and ITI Small Cap Fund also saw one-year returns of 59.54 percent and 58.61 percent, respectively.

In the flexi-cap category, Motilal Oswal Flexi Cap Fund delivered a 6.59 percent return for the month and 62.83 percent for the year. Other key performers include Axis Flexi Cap Fund (3.51 percent for the month and 41.49 percent annually) and UTI Flexi Cap Fund (3.40 percent for the month, 30.63 percent annually).

In the multi-cap category, Motilal Oswal Multi Cap Fund reported a 6.37 percent monthly return, followed by Aditya Birla SL Multi-Cap Fund (3.56 percent) and SBI Multicap Fund (3.53 percent). For the one-year period, the Axis Multicap Fund emerged as a top performer with a 56.17 percent return, reflecting the broader market’s strong recovery across sectors.

Amongst the top 10 SIP funds over the past year that have yielded exceptional returns, Bandhan Small Cap Fund leads with an XIRR of 81.28 percent, followed by Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund (73.97 percent) and LIC MF Dividend Yield Fund (68.18 percent).
